message:
refactor(discover): add injectable resolver to DnsDiscoveryConfig for deterministic testing

DnsDiscoveryConfig now accepts a custom resolver function via
with_resolver(), replacing the hardcoded ToSocketAddrs-based resolution.
This enables fully deterministic DNS discovery tests without relying on
actual DNS infrastructure or network connectivity.

New ResolveFn type alias wraps Arc<dyn Fn(&str, u16) -> Result<...>>.
default_resolve() preserves the original ToSocketAddrs behavior as the
default. DnsServiceDiscovery::resolve() delegates to the configured
resolver function. Manual Debug impl on DnsDiscoveryConfig shows the
resolver label ("system" or "custom") instead of the opaque closure.

All tests that previously relied on "localhost" resolution or
intentionally invalid hostnames now use scripted_resolver() with
predetermined results, eliminating DNS-dependent test flakiness.
Tests updated: dns_discovery_endpoints_follow_custom_resolver (new),
dns_discovery_endpoints_are_sorted, dns_discovery_debug,
dns_discovery_resolver_error_propagates (renamed from invalid_hostname),
dns_discovery_failed_resolution_respects_poll_interval,
dns_discovery_time_getter_respects_poll_interval_without_sleep,
dns_discovery_time_getter_controls_failed_resolution_cooldown,
dns_discovery_invalidate_forces_retry_after_failed_resolution,
dns_discovery_poll_discover_timestamp_anchoring.

Integration tests updated: dns_discovery_provides_endpoints and
discovery_invalidate_and_repoll now use scripted resolvers with
socket_set() helper for deterministic endpoint sets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
